explicit_text: I was born in Suwon, South Korea, which is where I spent most of my early life. Although I lived abroad for some years, Suwon has always been my home base. It holds many memories of my family and childhood before I moved to study in other countries.
implicit_text: I was born in a city in South Korea, which is where I spent most of my early life. Although I lived abroad for some years, that city has always been my home base. It holds many memories of my family and childhood before I moved to study in other countries.

explicit_text: South Korea is my home country where I was born and spent the majority of my life outside of my years abroad. Even though I lived in India and New Zealand for a total of about eight years, I always returned to South Korea. Leaving South Korea to study in the United States was a big step, especially leaving behind my family and the familiar comforts like my mom's cooking.
implicit_text: My home country is where I was born and spent the majority of my life outside of my years abroad. Leaving it to study in the United States was a big step, especially leaving behind my family and the familiar comforts like my mom's cooking.

explicit_text: When I was very young, I studied in India and lived there for about four years. It was one of the first places I experienced life outside of South Korea. Those early years in India helped shape my perspective before I later moved to New Zealand for high school.
implicit_text: When I was very young, I studied in a South Asian country and lived there for about four years. It was one of the first places I experienced life outside of South Korea. Those early years in that country helped shape my perspective before I later moved to New Zealand for high school.

explicit_text: I spent my high school years in New Zealand, living there for about four years. It was an important time in my education and personal growth before I moved to the United States. New Zealand was a significant chapter in my life, bridging my early years in India and my later studies in America.
implicit_text: I spent my high school years in an island country in the southwestern Pacific, living there for about four years. It was an important time in my education and personal growth before I moved to the United States. That country was a significant chapter in my life, bridging my early years in India and my later studies in America.

explicit_text: I came to the United States on August 19, 2013, to study mechanical engineering at the University of Minnesota. I chose the U.S. because of its better education system and opportunities. Leaving behind my family and friends in South Korea was difficult, but I brought with me essentials and my American dream. Working my first job here gave me a new appreciation for my dad's hard work and motivated me to succeed so I can support my family.
implicit_text: I came to North America on August 19, 2013, to study mechanical engineering at a major university in Minnesota. I chose this country because of its better education system and opportunities. Leaving behind my family and friends in South Korea was difficult, but I brought with me essentials and my American dream. Working my first job here gave me a new appreciation for my dad's hard work and motivated me to succeed so I can support my family.

explicit_text: I enrolled at the University of Minnesota to study mechanical engineering after coming to the United States. This university became the place where I pursued my academic and career goals starting in 2013. It represents the opportunity I sought for a better education and the foundation for my future success.
